name: AI Ecosystem
description: Top-level: visible AI applications and the demand they create.
why_it_matters: Drives all downstream demand for compute and physical infrastructure.
notes: Demand layer; not itself a bottleneck.

name: AI applications & end-user demand
description: Consumer chatbots; copilots; agents; vertical AI apps.

name: Foundation models & model developers
description: Frontier and open-weight model labs and their successors.
notes: Concentration of capability among a few labs.

name: Training & inference platforms
why_it_matters: Performance and cost of every model run.
notes: Mostly software; cheap to substitute in principle.

name: Cloud hyperscalers
description: AWS; Azure; GCP; OCI; Tencent; Alibaba.
why_it_matters: Largest single buyers of GPU and physical infra.

name: GPU cloud / neocloud providers
description: CoreWeave; Lambda; Crusoe; Nebius; Voltage Park.
why_it_matters: Faster expansion path than hyperscaler self-build for some customers.

name: AI accelerators & processors
description: GPUs; TPUs; custom ASICs; CPUs; FPGAs; DPUs.
notes: NVDA dominant on GPU; bottlenecks downstream.

name: Semiconductor design
description: Fabless designers and IDMs that design AI chips.
why_it_matters: Translates demand into physical chip designs.

name: Semiconductor IP
description: Arm; RISC-V vendors; PHY/IP houses (Synopsys/Cadence IP).
why_it_matters: IP licensing is a structural toll.
notes: Arm near-monopoly on mobile-class compute IP.

name: EDA software
description: Design tools to make chips physically realizable.
why_it_matters: Cannot tape out a leading-edge chip without it.
notes: Effective duopoly Synopsys / Cadence (plus Siemens EDA).

name: Wafer fabrication & foundries
why_it_matters: Single-largest physical bottleneck for advanced chips.

name: Advanced packaging
why_it_matters: Required to integrate HBM with logic.

name: DRAM; HBM; NAND
description: Memory used in AI training and inference.

name: Optical interconnects
notes: Coherent / Lumentum / Fabrinet at the center.

name: Servers; OEM/ODM
notes: Many integrators; ODMs concentrated in Taiwan.

name: Storage systems
notes: WDC / STX / NetApp; flash is more bottlenecked.

name: Data-center operators
notes: Power and land are gating factors.

name: Data-center REITs
why_it_matters: Public-equity expression of physical DC growth.

name: Transformers; switchgear (grid-side)
why_it_matters: Lead times now multi-year for large units.
notes: Foreign supply concentrated; U.S. capacity scarce.

name: Cooling & thermal management
why_it_matters: Required to dissipate dense GPU heat.

name: Immersion cooling
why_it_matters: Possible future standard for very dense racks.

name: Water supply & treatment
why_it_matters: Water rights are a permitting issue.

name: Grid interconnection
why_it_matters: Multi-year queues in PJM / ERCOT-N / CAISO.
notes: Process bottleneck not a company bottleneck.

name: Natural-gas power
why_it_matters: Fastest add for new MW; turbine-supply constrained.
notes: GE Vernova / Mitsubishi / Siemens turbines on backlog.

name: Transmission & distribution equipment
why_it_matters: Get power from generation to DC.
notes: Long lead times across most categories.

name: Fuel supply chains
notes: Uranium and HALEU are notable choke points.

name: Semiconductor manufacturing equipment
description: Litho; deposition; etch; clean; metrology; implant.

name: Critical minerals & raw materials
why_it_matters: Root inputs to chips and DCs.

name: Mining
why_it_matters: Volume layer for copper; aluminum; lithium etc.

name: Aluminum
why_it_matters: Cheaper substitute for some Cu uses.

name: Silicon / polysilicon
notes: Wacker / Hemlock / GCL; not directly U.S.-listed.

name: Gallium
description: GaN HEMTs; RF; LEDs; some power.

name: Germanium
description: Optical fiber; IR optics; some compound semis.

name: Neon; argon; krypton; xenon
notes: LIN / APD merchant; supply diversifying post-2022.

name: Permitting; environmental review; local politics
notes: Process bottleneck not a company bottleneck.
